The prophet is a watchman for Israel. His job is to warn sinners about their misery and danger. He must warn the wicked to turn from their ways so they may live. If souls perish because he fails in his duty, he brings guilt upon himself. See what those have to answer for who make excuses for sin, flatter sinners, and encourage them to believe they will have peace even though they continue on. How much wiser people are in their everyday concerns than in their spiritual ones! They set watchmen to guard their houses and sentinels to warn of the enemy's approach, but when it comes to the everlasting happiness or misery of the soul, they are upset if ministers obey their Master’s command and give a faithful warning; they would rather perish while listening to pleasant things.

Those who despaired of finding mercy with God receive an answer in a solemn declaration of God's readiness to show mercy. The destruction of the city and state was decided, but that did not relate to the final condition of people. God says to the righteous person that he will surely live. But many who have made a profession have been ruined by proud confidence in themselves. People trust in their own righteousness, and presuming on their own sufficiency, they are brought to commit sin. If those who have lived a wicked life repent and forsake their wicked ways, they will be saved. Many such amazing and blessed changes have been accomplished by the power of Divine grace. When there is a settled separation between a person and sin, there will no longer be a separation between him and God.

Those are truly unteachable who do not learn their dependence upon God when all comforts fail. Many claim an interest in the special blessings given to true believers, while their actions prove they are enemies of God. They call this groundless presumption strong faith, when God’s testimony declares them entitled to his threats and nothing else.

Unworthy and corrupt motives often lead people to places where the word of God is faithfully preached. Many come to find something to oppose; far more come out of curiosity or mere habit. People's hearts may be changed. But whether people hear or not, they will know by what happens that a servant of God has been among them. All who do not recognize the value of mercies through using them properly will justly be made to recognize their worth through lacking them.
